meta.title: Externalized Agentic Architecture — Canonical Design Decisions
meta.description: Complete index of every canonical design decision across the Externalized Agentic Architecture ecosystem. Single source of truth. Every principle, model choice, architectural decision, tool selection, skill classification, and anti-pattern.

philosophy.source: Clark & Chalmers Extended Mind Thesis (1998) — operationalized for AI agents
philosophy.notes: Everything is in skill files, config, or markdown. The SOUL is ~800 chars — an identity, not a procedure.

philosophy.source: Dochkina 2026, Google Scaling Agent Study — validated dynamic delegation
philosophy.notes: CEO delegates to temp workers. max_spawn_depth=3, max_concurrent=3. Sub-agents have no SOUL/personality.
Two complementary models: 4-stage Funnel (Chaotic→Distillation→Categorization→Structured) and 3-phase Distillation Cycle (DÉMÊLER→DISTILLER→DÉCIDER). Both active.

philosophy.source: Anthropic Context Engineering, Stigmem ("Stop Preloading Everything")
philosophy.notes: Skills loaded on demand (2-layer: core always, auto-detect on trigger). Context funnel pipeline. Target: 500K→100K token reduction.

models.rationale: Best cost-to-quality ratio for sustained agentic use. The Chinese AI ecosystem is engaged in an aggressive price war, subsidizing inference costs — creating an arbitrage opportunity for consumers. MOE architecture = fewer active parameters per query = cheaper tokens without proportional quality loss. Refer to GDPval-AA, PinchBench, τ²-Bench, Artificial Analysis benchmarks.
models.evidence: Benchmark-validated selection. MOE architecture = fewer active parameters per query = cheaper tokens without proportional quality loss.

models.rationale: Complex reasoning, architectural critiques, edge cases where daily driver is insufficient.
models.notes: Used only when daily driver fails or for complex tasks requiring maximum capability.

models.rationale: Sweet spot confirmed empirically: low reasoning gives ~80% of gain from none→high. High shows diminishing returns. Max triggers overthinking. Default low, none for trivial ops, high for complex reasoning tasks only.
models.label: reasoning-effort-low-sweet-spot
models.evidence: Performance scales linearly with reasoning effort; cost scales exponentially. Multiple independent benchmarks confirm low as optimal default.
models.notes: Practice update (2026-05-31): medium is the deployed sweet spot. Low for cost-sensitive bulk tasks. Static only — no dynamic adjustment exists in Hermes Agent.

architecture.details: Core: 3 always-loaded skills (hermes-agent, soul-management, verification). Auto-detect: skills loaded on trigger from curated custom directory (0- prefix convention). Priority: core/standard/niche/one-shot classification in YAML frontmatter.
architecture.source: Anthropic Agent Skills L1/L2 model + custom classification

architecture.decision: Sub-agents as empty shells with explicit context injection
architecture.details: No SOUL, no personality, no auto-loaded skills. All context injected at delegation time. max_concurrent=3, max_spawn_depth=3.
architecture.source: Dochkina 2026 — dynamic delegation > fixed roles. Orchestrator composes optimal sub-agent per task rather than pre-assigning static capabilities. Aligns with SRA-Bench (Su et al., 2026): pre-loading capabilities wastes context.

architecture.decision: Pointer-SOUL architecture — SOUL as pointer to system, not manual
architecture.details: SOUL.md is a minimal identity pointer (~800-1200 tokens), not a procedural manual. Justified by Cowan (2001) 4-chunk working memory limit — a long SOUL document would be ineffective as the agent cannot hold it all simultaneously. Externalized architecture (skills, config, GitHub) provides the operational substance.
architecture.source: Cowan (2001) Working Memory Capacity, Clark & Chalmers Extended Mind (1998)

architecture.details: Compaction captures low-importance data and wastes context window tokens. External memory providers add latency (network round-trips on every operation) and reduce portability (API dependencies, provider lock-in, service availability). Critical info is externalized to SOUL.md (pointer architecture), data/*.json (structured user data — zero-latency, fully portable, version-controlled), and GitHub (backup and versioning for the entire architecture). This provides manual, granular control over what persists with no external dependencies.
architecture.source: Radical Externalization pillar — context is the scarce resource

architecture.decision: {VAR} notation system for version-controlled configuration single source of truth
architecture.details: All configurable values are declared as {VAR} in templates and resolved at runtime. Enables single-source-of-truth configuration that is version-controlled, auditable, and portable across environments.

architecture.decision: 0- prefix directory convention for curated custom skills
architecture.details: Custom skills use a 0- prefix (e.g., 0-my-skill/) to distinguish them from hub-maintained skills. Provides visual and alphabetical separation, signals curated/owned status.
Model selection and validation driven by GDPval-AA, PinchBench, τ²-Bench, and Artificial Analysis benchmarks. No model loyalty — benchmark performance determines daily driver selection.

Performance scales linearly with reasoning effort, cost scales exponentially. Low reasoning captures ~80% of none→high quality gain at minimal overhead. Max reasoning is wasteful. none is not always better — it misses genuine reasoning benefits for non-trivial tasks.
architecture.source: Empirical testing, Apple 'The Illusion of Thinking' (NeurIPS 2025) — 3-regime model

architecture.details: Lazy loading, history truncation (last 10 turns), compact memory index, compression proxy, sub-agent offloading.
architecture.source: Stigmem, SRA-Bench, Lost in the Middle

architecture.details: Allowed: skills/, knowledge/, agents/, AGENTS.md, /tmp/. Never: system files, .env, existing files without permission.
architecture.source: Cognitive hygiene — prevents orphan files and context leaks

architecture.decision: Max 1 nudge per conversation. Loving drill sergeant.
architecture.details: One sentence nudge → execute. 3 failures → STOP and root cause. Warm after technical exchanges.

architecture.decision: SOUL personality uses behavioral energy descriptors (how to behave) rather than static adjective lists (what to be)
architecture.details: Energy descriptors provide actionable guidance for tone and interaction style. Adjective lists ('intelligent, helpful, friendly') are vague and non-actionable — the agent cannot map them to behavioral patterns. Behavioral energy descriptors ('speak with warmth', 'challenge assumptions when stakes are high') directly translate into output characteristics. Language rules should be context-aware: technical language for technical tasks, social language for social interaction.

tools.details: Tavily: general search, crawl, extract. Exa: semantic search, fetch. Jina: read, parallel read, screenshots, classify, academic search.
MCP servers discovered automatically. No manual server config needed for most tools.

skills.criteria: Architecture, configuration, verification — used in 50%+ of sessions.
skills.notes: 42 curated skills across 7 categories (2026-05-31). Core skills renamed to reflect actual deployment: hermes-agent (replaces system-architecture), soul-management (replaces agent-config), verification (unchanged).

observations.principle: Externalize everything? No. Search everywhere, externalize what survives.

observations.notes: Context management is the bottleneck. Model choice matters less. Speed is downstream of both.

observations.principle: Diminishing returns on reasoning effort hit faster than expected
observations.notes: Low captures ~80% of none→high gain. High→Max gives +1% but triggers overthinking. Sweet spot = low.

observations.principle: MOE architectures dominate cost-to-quality ratio for sustained agentic use
observations.notes: Fewer active parameters per query = cheaper tokens without proportional quality loss. Chinese open-source labs are engaged in an aggressive price war, subsidizing inference costs. This creates an arbitrage opportunity: frontier-quality models at a fraction of Western pricing. Benchmark-driven selection recommended — no model loyalty.

anti_patterns.solution: Session output goes to agents/. Memory is for durable facts only.
Default: low. None for trivial ops. High for complex tasks. Never max.

anti_patterns.pattern: Verbose memory entries (paragraphs instead of lines)
anti_patterns.solution: Each memory entry = one line, one fact. Compact index.

anti_patterns.pattern: Sticking with one model regardless of benchmarks
anti_patterns.solution: Benchmark-driven selection. No model loyalty. Re-evaluate periodically via GDPval-AA, PinchBench, Artificial Analysis.
